setup: if command -v gh >/dev/null 2>&1 && gh auth status >/dev/null 2>&1; then gh repo set-default ByteTrue/paseo >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi

desktop.command: cross-env PASEO_DEV_MANAGED_HOME=1 PASEO_DEV_ROOT="$PASEO_WORKTREE_PATH" PASEO_HOME="$PASEO_WORKTREE_PATH/.dev/paseo-home" PASEO_LISTEN=0.0.0.0:${PASEO_SERVICE_DAEMON_PORT} PASEO_DEV_DAEMON_ENDPOINT=localhost:${PASEO_SERVICE_DAEMON_PORT} EXPO_PORT=$PASEO_PORT npm run dev --workspace=@bytetrue/desktop

check.command: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run typecheck

codegraph.command: if command -v codegraph >/dev/null 2>&1; then codegraph init -i; else echo 'codegraph is not installed'; exit 1; fi

agentTitle.instructions: Generate a short task title for this Paseo fork. Prefer the touched area or package name first (server, app, desktop, cli, relay, website, docs), then the user-visible intent. Keep it under 7 words. No emoji, no generic titles like 'Paseo Update'.

branchName.instructions: Use lowercase kebab-case with a clear prefix: feat/, fix/, docs/, refactor/, chore/, release/, or upstream-sync/. Keep branch names concise and specific. This fork works in ByteTrue/paseo; do not name branches as if they target getpaseo/paseo unless explicitly syncing/cherry-picking upstream.

commitMessage.instructions: Use Conventional Commit style when it fits (feat:, fix:, docs:, refactor:, chore:, test:). Keep the subject imperative and under 72 characters. Mention important validation in the body for non-trivial changes. Respect fork constraints: browser web + Electron only; do not reintroduce native mobile release/build surface unless explicitly requested.

pullRequest.instructions: Target ByteTrue/paseo main, not upstream getpaseo/paseo. Include Summary, Validation, and Risk/Compatibility notes. Call out daemon/client protocol or security implications when touched. If a change intentionally ignores old-client compatibility because this fork has a single user, state that explicitly. Mention if no native iOS/Android surface was changed.
